mnemonics: Past Patterns Predict Poorly (the 4 P's of Overfitting) - A model that chases every past nuance fails on new future data.
keyPoints: Overfitting occurs when a model learns the noise and random fluctuations in the training data instead of the underlying trend.
An overfit model will have excellent performance on the training data but poor performance on out-of-sample test data or forecasts.
Prevention techniques include using simpler models, cross-validation techniques designed for time series (like rolling-forward windows), and regularization (e.g., LASSO).
commonMistakes: Using standard K-Fold cross-validation on time series data, which breaks the temporal order and creates data leakage, giving over-optimistic results.
Adding too many lags or exogenous variables without justification, increasing model complexity and the risk of fitting noise.
Evaluating model performance only on training data metrics (like R-squared) and not on a hold-out test set.
